behavior_summary: When the player examines the tapestry object (the engine invokes the object's U8P_TAPESTEW::look process), the script emits a single short bark/text: "tapestry".
After producing the bark the process suspends/pauses, indicating the U8P_TAPESTEW::look/description interaction ends with no state changes, branching, or external calls.
notable_control_flow: U8P_TAPESTEW::look runs as a process-type handler: it issues one Bark node with the text "tapestry" and then immediately suspends — a straight-line, single-output flow.
No conditional branches, menus, or function calls alter execution; the function is minimal and terminates by suspension.
open_questions: The original disassembly contains two 'Unknown' nodes; what precise engine-level instructions those represent (timing, minor side-effects, or decompiler remnants) is unclear but they do not appear to change observable behavior.
Is this U8P_TAPESTEW::look handler tied to any fast-area enter/leave triggers in the map (metadata infers a U8P_TAPESTEW::look/examine invocation but not the exact in-world trigger region)?
